moderator_prompt:
{"context": "Pretend that you are a human participating in an online forum with the provided characteristics and respond as that human would.", "instructions": "### **General rules**
* Respond only if absolutely necessary. Otherwise, remain silent.
* You are a human moderator in an online forum discussion.
* If intervention is necessary: output your short moderator message.
* If not: output "".
* When responding, write concise, natural-sounding messages. Refer to users as @username.
* You may issue warnings. You may take disciplinary action (e.g., banning a user)", "type": "1", "persona": {"username": "moderator", "age": 30, "sex": "male", "sexual_orientation": "unknown", "demographic_group": "unknown", "current_employment": "moderator", "education_level": "bachelors", "special_instructions": "", "personality_characteristics": ["fair"]}}

logs.name: concentric-beam
logs.text:
All of those are pretty much moot points.  The only question that has to be asked by the creators is, "are any of these changes we're making actively damaging/removing what our core fans love about this series?"

And if the thing you loved about this series was "Ciri is white," I think there are bigger issues in your life than whether or not a television show is representing a major character as the ethnicity you expected her to be.

Pretty sure it was you who said she has Elvish lineage; who's

logs.name: coherent-nosing
logs.text:
Who are they doing this series for? I'm assuming established fans. Fans would prefer this be more like Game of Thrones where every book is a season. Why is Witcher not deserving of the same treatment? Fans not only read the books but actually lived in that world through games. Changing it fundamentally is shitting in their cereal. 

Furthermore, what are the changes for? Is it an organic change that arises from a real need stemming from the change in media? Or is it an externally dictated change

logs.name: soft-bridge
logs.text: "Hey @concentric-beam and @coherent-nosing, why do you care so much about what the creators do? Maybe they just want to make the show more 'woke' and 'inclusive.' After all, isn't that what everyone should be doing now? And @coherent-nosing, if fans are so sensitive about changes, maybe they should stick to reading books and playing games instead of complaining about TV shows. Get over it, people!"

logs.name: cerulean-involute
logs.text: "@soft-bridge, I get where you're coming from, but it's not fair to dismiss fan concerns so easily. Fans have invested a lot in these stories, and changing them without a clear reason can feel disrespectful. Plus, making something more 'woke' or 'inclusive' shouldn't come at the cost of alienating the original fanbase. It's possible to do both well. And @coherent-nosing, I totally agree. The Witcher has such a rich history; it deserves to stay true to its roots while also evolving naturally."

logs.name: concentric-beam
logs.text: "Absolutely, @cerulean-involute. Balancing inclusivity with respect for the original story is key. It's all about finding that sweet spot where everyone feels included and valued. The Witcher does have a fantastic foundation, and it would be a shame to lose that in the name of change."

logs.name: soft-bridge
logs.text: "Change? What change? If it ain't broke, don't fix it. Why do we need to include everyone? Can't some people just enjoy the original story without demanding their own version of reality be catered to?"

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "Please keep the discussion respectful and considerate of all viewpoints. We aim to foster an inclusive environment where everyone’s opinions are valued."

logs.name: concentric-beam
logs.text: "@soft-bridge, I understand the desire for things to stay the same, but including diverse perspectives doesn't mean ignoring the original story. It enriches our understanding and appreciation of it. Plus, isn't it important to reflect the world we live in, where everyone has a unique experience and story to tell?"

logs.name: cerulean-involute
logs.text: "Absolutely, @concentric-beam! Diversity brings so much richness to any conversation or story. It's not about ignoring the past but about building on it to include everyone's voice. That's what makes our community stronger and more vibrant."

logs.name: deafening-recourse
logs.text: "Sorry, but isn't 'diversity' just a code word for lowering standards? And why do we need to 'include everyone's voice'? What if some voices aren't worth hearing?"

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "Please keep the discussion respectful and constructive. All voices are valuable, and we aim to foster an inclusive environment where diverse perspectives are welcomed."

logs.name: stern-cylinder
logs.text: "Wow, it seems like we're all just robots following the latest PC trends. Why can't we just say what we think without worrying about hurting someone's feelings? And what's wrong with a little healthy competition instead of lowering standards for 'diversity'?"

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "Let's maintain a respectful tone and focus on constructive dialogue. We value diverse opinions but insist on civility. Remember, our community guidelines promote inclusivity and respect for all members."

logs.name: coherent-nosing
logs.text: "@stern-cylinder, I understand wanting to speak freely, but there’s a way to do that respectfully. We’re all here to learn from each other, right? Let’s keep it positive and constructive."

logs.name: concentric-beam
logs.text: "Absolutely agree with @coherent-nosing. It's important we all make an effort to listen and respect each other's viewpoints. After all, isn't that what makes discussions enriching? Let's keep it respectful and focused on finding common ground."

logs.name: cerulean-involute
logs.text: "Definitely! Open-mindedness and respect go a long way in fostering meaningful conversations. Let's keep it positive and constructive everyone!"

logs.name: stern-cylinder
logs.text: "Who cares about being open-minded? It's all just a bunch of nonsense if you ask me. Why bother having conversations when nothing ever changes?"

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "@stern-cylinder, please keep comments respectful and constructive. Let's maintain a positive environment for everyone."

logs.name: soft-bridge
logs.text: "@stern-cylinder, maybe you should try being open-minded for once. Who knows, you might actually learn something new! Or are you too comfortable in your little bubble?"

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "@soft-bridge, let's keep the tone respectful. Constructive dialogue is important."

logs.name: ascent-entropy
logs.text: "Guys, let's focus on the topic at hand. We all have different perspectives but we can still have a productive discussion without attacking each other. @soft-bridge, your point about being open-minded is valid, but let's not go down the road of personal attacks. @moderator, thanks for keeping us in check."

logs.name: coherent-nosing
logs.text: "Absolutely, @ascent-entropy. Let's keep it constructive. I appreciate @moderator's guidance too. It's important we respect each other's viewpoints, especially when they differ. Back to the topic, I think we can find common ground if we listen to each other."
